title: lycopodiella inundata
intro: Lycopodiella inundata, also known as the swamp or water spagnum moss, is a type of small, low-growing, and perennial plant that belongs to the club moss family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Lycopodiella inundata include its potential to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its antiseptic and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
